description: Main learning videos for performing addition, subtraction, and comparison calculations using real village data and scenarios

videos.title: Using Village Data for Calculations | Adding and Subtracting Real Numbers
videos.description: Learn how to take numbers from village tables and use them in addition and subtraction. If a village planted 256 trees and 189 were neem trees, how many were other types?
learningObjectives: Extract numbers from tables for calculations
Perform addition and subtraction with village data
Connect data reading to arithmetic operations

questions.question: A village planted 312 trees in Park A and 189 trees in Park B. How many trees in total?

videos.title: Comparing Village Data | Who Collected More? How Much More?
videos.description: Learn to compare data from different villages or different days. Find out who collected more rubbish, how much more one village planted, and what the difference is.
learningObjectives: Compare numbers from two or more data sources
Find the difference between two quantities
Use subtraction to answer 'how much more' questions

questions.question: Village A collected 425 bags and Village B collected 368 bags. How many more bags did Village A collect?

videos.title: Step-by-Step Village Calculations | Worked Examples
videos.description: Watch step-by-step solutions to village calculation problems. See how to read the table, pick out the right numbers, choose the right operation, and solve carefully.
learningObjectives: Follow a step-by-step approach to solve calculation problems
Choose the correct operation for each situation

videos.title: Village Calculation Practice | Try It Yourself!
videos.description: Practice problems using village data. Pause the video, try each problem yourself, then check your answer. Problems get harder as you go!

videos.title: Mawlynnong: India's Cleanest Village | Maths in Action
videos.description: Discover how Mawlynnong village in Meghalaya uses counting and data to stay clean. See real numbers and learn how maths helps make a village the cleanest in Asia!
learningObjectives: See real-world application of village math calculations
Understand how data and calculations help communities
Be inspired by India's cleanest village

standard.description: For students who scored >= 80% on readiness

with_prerequisites.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness

questions.question: Street A has 178 dustbins and Street B has 245 dustbins. How many more dustbins does Street B have?
questions.explanation: 245 - 178 = 67. Street B has 67 more dustbins than Street A.

ifCorrect.message: Great work! You can compare village data and find differences.

ifWrong.reason: Student needs subtraction review before continuing with comparisons

usageNotes.purpose: Teach students to perform calculations using data from village and community scenarios
usageNotes.recommendationStrategy: Select playlist path based on readiness assessment score
usageNotes.sequencing: Data calculations intro → Comparing data → Worked examples → Independent practice → Real-world inspiration
